projectDescription: A malicious traffic detection system that monitors network traffic against curated blacklists and heuristic rules, alerting on suspicious domains, IPs, and URLs via a web reporting interface.
keyFeatures: Real-time network traffic monitoring via passive packet capture
100+ threat intelligence feeds (blacklisted IPs, domains, URLs)
Heuristic detection for unknown threats (port scans, DNS exhaustion, DGA domains)
Web-based reporting interface with timeline, charts, and event filtering
Distributed Sensor → Server architecture supporting multiple sensors
Fail2ban and ipset integration for automated IP blocking
Custom trails support for user-defined threat indicators
publicApiSurface: sensor.py — CLI entry point for the network capture sensor
server.py — CLI entry point for the reporting HTTP server
GET /fail2ban — Attacker IP extraction endpoint for firewall integration
UDP <UDP_ADDRESS>:<UDP_PORT> — Remote log event collection from sensors
maltrail.conf — Central configuration file for all components
trails/feeds/*.py — Pluggable threat intelligence feed modules
